title: Resources
text: A curated collection of open resources I find useful in my daily research. Contributions and suggestions welcome — open an issue.

title: Resources
text:
Molecular & chemical databases



Database
Description
Link




ChEMBL
Bioactivity data for drug-like molecules
chembl.ebi.ac.uk


PubChem
Chemical structures, properties, bioassays
pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov


ZINC
Commercially available compounds for docking
zinc.docking.org


DrugBank
Drug targets, interactions, pharmacology
drugbank.com


BindingDB
Measured protein-ligand binding affinities
bindingdb.org


PDB
Experimental protein structures
rcsb.org


UniProt
Protein sequences and functional annotation
uniprot.org

title: Resources
text:
Toxicology & safety resources



Resource
Description
Link




ToxCast / CompTox
EPA HTS data for 10,000+ chemicals
comptox.epa.gov


hERG Central
Curated hERG activity database
chembl


vNN-ADMET
ADMET prediction server
vnnadmet.bhsai.org


SwissADME
ADME/drug-likeness predictor
swissadme.ch


ADMETlab 2.0
Comprehensive ADMET evaluation
admetmesh.scbdd.com


DSSTox
Distributed Structure Searchable Toxicity DB
comptox.epa.gov

title: Resources
text:
Key Python libraries








Library
Use
Install




RDKit
Cheminformatics swiss-army knife
conda install -c conda-forge rdkit


DeepChem
ML for drug discovery
pip install deepchem


MDAnalysis
MD trajectory analysis
pip install MDAnalysis


OpenMM
GPU MD simulations
conda install -c conda-forge openmm


py3Dmol
3D molecular visualization
pip install py3Dmol


LangChain
LLM application framework
pip install langchain


LangGraph
Agentic AI workflows
pip install langgraph


biopython
Bioinformatics utilities
pip install biopython


pydeseq2
DESeq2 in Python
pip install pydeseq2

text:
Recommended books

Introduction to Computational Chemistry — Frank Jensen
The Art of Drug Synthesis — Johnson & Li

title: Tutorials
text: All tutorials are Jupyter notebooks in the computational-science-tutorials GitHub repo. Click Open in Colab to run any notebook instantly in your browser — no installation required.

title: Tutorials
text:
Drug design & CADD









#
Tutorial
Description
Run




1
Molecular fingerprints & Tanimoto similarity
RDKitFingerprintsSimilarity
Morgan fingerprints, ECFP, Tanimoto similarity, and building your first compound library screen



2
Lipinski Ro5 & ADMET profiling
ADMETDrug-likenessRDKit
Screen compound libraries for drug-likeness — MW, LogP, HBD, HBA, TPSA — before expensive docking



3
Virtual screening with AutoDock Vina
DockingVinaVirtual Screening
Set up and run a docking campaign against a PDB target using Python wrappers around AutoDock Vina



4
hERG cardiotoxicity prediction
hERGSafetyML
Build an ML model combining physicochemical descriptors to flag hERG blockers early in drug design

title: Tutorials
text:
Cheminformatics









#
Tutorial
Description
Run




5
RDKit from scratch
RDKitBasicsDescriptors
Load, draw, and compute properties of molecules — the essential starting point for all cheminformatics



6
Chemical space visualization with UMAP
UMAPPCAChemical Space
Visualize thousands of compounds in 2D using PCA and UMAP of Morgan fingerprints with matplotlib



7
SMARTS substructure search
SMARTSSubstructureFunctional Groups
Write SMARTS patterns to search for functional groups, ring systems, and pharmacophore features

title: Tutorials
text:
Bioinformatics & toxicogenomics









#
Tutorial
Description
Run




8
RNA-Seq QC & differential expression
RNA-SeqDESeq2PyDESeq2
End-to-end pipeline from raw count matrix through DESeq2 differential expression and volcano plot



9
In vitro–in vivo correlation (IVIVE)
IVIVEToxicologyCross-species
Correlate gene expression changes between animal models and human hepatocytes for translational toxicology



10
MEA neurotoxicity & dose-response modeling
MEANeurotoxDose-Response
Process Multi-Electrode Array data and fit dose-response curves with tcplfit2 for chemical neurotoxicity

title: Tutorials
text:
Agentic AI for science









#
Tutorial
Description
Run




11
RAG pipeline over scientific literature
RAGLangChainPubMed
Build a retrieval-augmented system that answers drug-target questions from your paper corpus + PubMed



12
LangGraph agent: ChEMBL + PubChem
LangGraphChEMBLAgentic AI
A multi-step agent that retrieves structures, queries ChEMBL bioactivity data, and summarizes findings

title: Tutorials
text:
Machine learning & deep learning









#
Tutorial
Description
Run




13
Classical ML baseline for QSAR
Random ForestXGBoostQSAR
Build RF and XGBoost classifiers for molecular activity — always establish this baseline before deep learning



14
Graph Neural Networks for molecules
GNNPyTorch GeometricDeepChem
Build a GCN treating molecules as graphs — atoms as nodes, bonds as edges — for property prediction



15
Fine-tuning ChemBERTa for toxicity
TransformerChemBERTaTransfer Learning
Fine-tune a pre-trained molecular transformer on a toxicity dataset using HuggingFace Transformers
Code

About me
I’m a computational scientist with 7+ years of interdisciplinary experience spanning computational chemistry, biology, and toxicology. My work sits at the intersection of AI/ML, drug discovery, and translational safety research.
Most recently I was a Computational Research Scientist II at BHSAI / Henry M. Jackson Foundation (Frederick, MD), working on DoD, DTRA, NIH, and AFRL funded projects. Before that I was a postdoctoral researcher in Dr. Alex MacKerell’s CADD Center at the University of Maryland, Baltimore, where I developed and applied the SILCS technology for protein-ligand binding and drug design.
I’m currently open to new opportunities in computational drug discovery, AI/ML for life sciences, or research scientist roles. Get in touch →
→ See my full education & research journey
→ 15 publications on Google Scholar

Expertise

Computer-Aided Drug Design Machine Learning / Deep Learning Graph Neural Networks LLM / Transformer models Agentic AI / LangGraph / RAG Generative AI for molecules RNA-Seq / Toxicogenomics Molecular Dynamics Monte Carlo Simulations DFT / Quantum Chemistry ADMET Profiling Cheminformatics / RDKit Multi-Electrode Array (MEA) MultiOMICS Python · R · Fortran · C++

Latest tutorials


🧬 #### Drug Design & CADD Virtual screening, molecular docking, SILCS FragMaps, pharmacophore modeling, and SAR analysis with Python. Browse tutorials →


⚗️ #### Cheminformatics RDKit from scratch, fingerprints, similarity search, SMARTS patterns, chemical space visualization. Browse tutorials →


🧫 #### Bioinformatics & RNA-Seq DESeq2, differential expression, pathway analysis, in vitro–in vivo correlation, cross-species analysis. Browse tutorials →


🤖 #### Agentic AI Building AI agents with LangGraph, LangChain, RAG pipelines, and tool-calling LLMs applied to science. Browse tutorials →


📊 #### ML & Multimodal GNNs, transformers for molecules, transfer learning, DeepChem, PyTorch for life-science tasks. Browse tutorials →

title: Code & GitHub
text:
All repositories live at github.com/himanshu-goel.
Each repo includes a README, example data, and a Jupyter notebook walkthrough.

title: Code & GitHub
text:
Drug discovery & CADD

🧬 silcs-drug-discovery
A Python workflow wrapping SILCS FragMap generation, pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening, and SAR analysis. Includes benchmarking against FEP results.
Key scripts: silcs_fragmap.py · virtual_screen.py · sar_analysis.py · binding_affinity.py
Tags: cadd silcs molecular-docking drug-discovery
View on GitHub →



⚡ herg-predictor
Machine learning pipeline to predict hERG1 blockade by combining SILCS-derived features with physicochemical properties. Based on the Chemistry 2022 paper.
Key scripts: featurize.py · train_herg_model.py · predict.py
Tags: herg cardiotoxicity machine-learning safety-pharmacology
View on GitHub →

title: Code & GitHub
text:
Toxicogenomics & bioinformatics

🧫 rnaseq-tox-pipeline
End-to-end RNA-Seq analysis pipeline for toxicogenomics studies. Covers QC, alignment, DESeq2 differential expression, pathway analysis, and in vitro–in vivo correlation. Validated on rat, guinea pig, and human hepatocyte data.
Key scripts: run_pipeline.sh · deseq2_analysis.R · pathway_enrichment.R · ivive_correlation.py
Tags: rna-seq toxicogenomics bioinformatics deseq2
View on GitHub →



📊 mea-neurotox
Analysis of Multi-Electrode Array (MEA) assay data for computational neurotoxicity profiling. Implements dose-response modeling via tcplfit2 and chemical-activity similarity analysis.
Key scripts: mea_preprocess.py · dose_response.R · activity_similarity.py · fingerprint_mea_correlation.py
Tags: neurotoxicity mea dose-response high-throughput
View on GitHub →

title: Code & GitHub
text:
Machine learning & AI

🤖 agentic-ai-drugdiscovery
Agentic AI workflows for drug discovery tasks using LangGraph, LangChain, and RAG. Includes tool-calling agents that query PubChem, ChEMBL, and UniProt, plus a RAG pipeline over scientific literature.
Key scripts: agent_drugquery.py · rag_pipeline.py · chembl_tool.py · pubchem_tool.py
Tags: agentic-ai langgraph rag llm drug-discovery
View on GitHub →



🔬 ml-compound-classifier
ML pipeline for classifying active/inactive compounds against neurotoxicity-relevant GPCR targets. Compares traditional ML (RF, XGBoost), DNNs, GNNs, and transformer-based LLMs using DeepChem and PyTorch.
Key scripts: featurize_compounds.py · train_rf_xgb.py · train_gnn.py · train_transformer.py · benchmark.py
Tags: machine-learning gnn transformer cheminformatics deepchem
View on GitHub →



🧪 generative-mol-design
Generative AI for novel molecule design — variational autoencoders, graph-based generative models, and LLM-guided molecular generation. Supports drug repurposing workflows.
Key scripts: vae_train.py · graph_gen.py · llm_mol_gen.py · admet_filter.py
Tags: generative-ai molecule-generation drug-repurposing vae
View on GitHub →

title: Code & GitHub
text:
Cheminformatics utilities

⚗️ cheminformatics-toolkit
A curated collection of RDKit-based utilities: fingerprint generation, chemical space PCA/UMAP, scaffold analysis, ADMET profiling wrappers (vNN-ADMET, SwissADME), and structure-activity relationship (SAR) visualization.
Key scripts: fingerprints.py · chemical_space.py · admet_profiling.py · sar_plot.py
Tags: rdkit cheminformatics admet chemical-space sar
View on GitHub →

title: Education & Research Journey
text:
🔬
    
      Feb 2022 – Mar 2025
      Computational Research Scientist II
      BHSAI · Henry M. Jackson Foundation for the Advancement of Military Medicine · Frederick, MD
      Led AI/ML-driven toxicology research on DoD, DTRA, NIH, and AFRL funded projects. Built neurotoxicity assessment pipelines, RNA-Seq toxicogenomics workflows, and deployed generative AI for drug repurposing.
    
  

  
    ⚗️
    
      May 2018 – Feb 2022
      Postdoctoral Researcher
      Computer-Aided Drug Design Center · University of Maryland, Baltimore · Advisor: Prof. Alexander D. MacKerell Jr.
      Developed and deployed SILCS-based computational drug discovery workflows. Created SILCS-MC Water for binding pocket water network prediction. Applied CADD to COVID-19 and hERG safety pharmacology.
    
  

  
    🎓
    
      Aug 2013 – May 2018
      PhD · Chemical Engineering
      Mississippi State University · Starkville, MS · Advisor: Prof. Neeraj Rai
      GPA 3.66 / 4.0
      Thesis: "Modeling Phase and Sorption Equilibria using First Principles Simulations" — Predicted vapor-liquid equilibria, developed force fields, and built Monte Carlo algorithms in CP2K for ionic liquids, hydrofluorocarbons, and environmental contaminants.
    
  

  
    🏛️
    
      Jul 2011 – Jul 2013
      M.Tech · Chemical Engineering
      Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad (IITH) · India
      CGPA 9.17 / 10.0 — Institute Rank 1
      Specialized in interfacial science and computational thermodynamics. Conducted dissipative particle dynamics simulations for interfacial properties of multi-liquid systems. Served as Teaching Assistant for Advanced Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics.
    
  

  
    🎖️
    
      Jun 2007 – Jun 2010
      B.E. · Chemical Engineering
      Bharati Vidyapeeth College of Engineering (BVCOE) · Pune, India
      Distinction · Silver Medal (2008)
      Top-ranked student. Won 1st Prize at MIT Pune for industry-defined problem on "Economical Desulfurization in Refineries" (HPCL sponsored). Won 1st Prize for paper on CO₂ sequestration using biomass at Bhartiyam 2010.

title: Education & Research Journey
text:
Research highlights

Drug design & CADD (UMB · 2018–2022)

  
    
    
      SILCS-based protein–ligand binding affinity
      Benchmarked SILCS relative binding affinities against FEP methods across multiple drug targets. Published in Chem. Sci. 2021 — one of my most-cited papers.
      SILCSFEPMD
    
  
  
    
    
      hERG cardiotoxicity prediction
      Combined SILCS FragMap features with physicochemical descriptors to predict hERG1 blockade — a critical safety filter in drug development. Published in Chemistry 2022.
      hERGSafetyML
    
  
  
    
    
      COVID-19 antiviral drug discovery
      Applied SILCS CADD workflow to SARS-CoV-2 spike protein as part of a rapid-response drug discovery team during the pandemic.
      COVID-19CADDAntiviral
    
  



Computational toxicology & AI/ML (BHSAI · 2022–2025)

  
    
    
      Toxicogenomics & RNA-Seq pipelines
      Built end-to-end RNA-Seq pipelines for in vivo (rat, guinea pig) and in vitro (human hepatocyte) toxicogenomics studies. Identified DEGs for liver and kidney injury biomarkers.
      RNA-SeqDESeq2IVIVE
    
  
  
    
    
      ML models for neurotoxicity (GPCR targets)
      Developed DNN, GNN, and transformer-based LLM classifiers for active/inactive compound classification against neurotoxicity-relevant GPCR targets from public databases.
      GNNLLMNeurotox
    
  
  
    
    
      Generative AI for drug repurposing
      Applied generative AI techniques to design novel molecular structures and support drug repurposing workflows, integrating LLM-guided generation with ADMET filters.
      Generative AILLMADMET
    
  



First-principles simulations (MSU PhD · 2013–2018)

  
    
    
      Vapor–liquid equilibria via DFT & Monte Carlo
      Predicted VLE, saturated vapor pressures, and critical properties for hydrofluorocarbons, CO₂, SO₂, and argon using first-principles Monte Carlo in CP2K. 7 publications.
      DFTMonte CarloCP2K
    
  
  
    
    
      CO₂ absorption in ionic liquids
      Predicted adsorption equilibria, transport properties, and structural mechanisms for CO₂ absorption in phosphonium-based ionic liquids using first-principles simulations.
      Ionic LiquidsCO₂DFT
    
  
  
    
    
      Force field development & environmental contaminants
      Developed TraPPE force fields for chloroethenes and computed octanol-water partition coefficients for NDMA and trichloroethylene — important environmental contaminants.
      TraPPEForce FieldEnvironment

title: Curriculum Vitae
text: 📄 Download PDF version   💼 LinkedIn   📧 hgchemical@gmail.com

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Professional summary
Experienced Computational Scientist with over 7 years of interdisciplinary experience in computational chemistry, biology, and toxicology. Strong background in computer-aided drug design, predictive toxicology, cheminformatics, bioinformatics, toxicogenomics, molecular dynamics, Monte Carlo, DFT, RNA-Seq, high-throughput screening, multiOMICS, and real-world datasets. Proficient in deep learning, transfer learning, generative AI, LLM, RAG, and agentic AI applied to real-world applications.

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Work experience

Computational Research Scientist II
BHSAI, Henry M. Jackson Foundation for the Advancement of Military Medicine · Frederick, MD
Feb 2022 – Mar 2025

Data curation & visualization of large-scale biological and chemical datasets (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP, clustering, heatmaps)
Generative AI for novel molecular structure generation and drug repurposing
ML models (DNN, GNN, transformer LLMs) for compound classification against neurotoxicity-relevant GPCR targets
Computational neurotoxicity assessment of 200+ chemicals via Multi-Electrode Array (MEA) assay data
Integrative toxicogenomics: RNA-Seq pipelines for in vivo/in vitro rat, guinea pig, and human hepatocyte data
DEG analysis for liver/kidney injury biomarkers; in vitro–in vivo correlation and cross-species analysis
ADMET profiling with vNN-ADMET/SwissADME for risk assessment
Funded by DoD, DTRA, NIH, and AFRL




Postdoctoral Researcher
Computer-Aided Drug Design Center, University of Maryland Baltimore · Baltimore, MD
May 2018 – Feb 2022
Advisor: Prof. Alexander D. MacKerell, Jr.

Developed and deployed computational workflows for early-phase drug discovery: protein prep, SILCS FragMaps, pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening, docking, SAR analysis
Benchmarked SILCS protein-ligand relative binding affinities against FEP methods
Designed SILCS workflow for polarizable force fields and MD simulations
Developed SILCS-MC Water — a novel technique to predict water networks in binding pockets
COVID-19: Applied SILCS CADD workflow targeting SARS-CoV-2 spike protein for antiviral drug development
hERG1 safety pharmacology: predictive models integrating SILCS + physicochemical properties




Research Assistant
Mississippi State University · Starkville, MS
Aug 2013 – May 2018
Advisor: Prof. Neeraj Rai

Predicted vapor-liquid equilibria (VLE), saturated vapor pressures, and critical properties via DFT/Monte Carlo
Adsorption equilibria, transport properties, and structural mechanisms for ionic liquids
Novel “cluster translation moves” algorithm in CP2K MC suite
TraPPE classical force field development for organic molecules
Octanol-water partition coefficients and solvation free energies for NDMA and trichloroethylene




Research Assistant
Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad · Hyderabad, India
Dec 2011 – Jul 2013

Interfacial properties for multiple liquid systems via dissipative particle dynamics
